title: Galloping into Film History

story: Cricket bats clashed with British rule in the hit movie 'Lagaan'. This Indian epic, starring Aamir Khan, bowled over audiences when it premiered on June 15, 2001. The film told a gripping story of villagers challenging their colonial rulers to a high-stakes cricket match. Its blend of sports drama and history made 'Lagaan' a home run with viewers worldwide.
activity_question: What sport is central to the plot of 'Lagaan'?
bonus_fact: 'Lagaan' means 'tax' in Hindi, reflecting the movie's plot about unfair taxation by British rulers.

title: A Musical Genius Born in Norway
story: Imagine a baby boy crying in Bergen, Norway. That tiny voice would grow into beautiful music! Edvard Grieg was born on June 15, 1843. He became a famous composer, creating magical melodies that still enchant listeners today. Grieg's most beloved work, 'Bewitched One,' captures the spirit of Norwegian folk tales and nature.
activity_question: What was Edvard Grieg's profession?
bonus_fact: Grieg's music often features trolls and other creatures from Norwegian folklore!

title: A Champion for Justice in South Africa
story: Trevor Huddleston roared into the world on June 15, 1913, in Bedford, England. He grew up to become a powerful voice against apartheid in South Africa. As an Anglican bishop, Huddleston fought tirelessly for equality. He even wrote a book called 'Naught for Your Comfort' to shine a light on the unfair treatment of people in South Africa.
activity_question: What book did Trevor Huddleston write?
follow_up_question: Why do you think Trevor Huddleston chose to fight against apartheid?

title: Hilda Terry: Trailblazing Cartoonist
story: Pencils and pens in hand, Hilda Terry sketched her way into history. Born on June 15, 1914, in Newburyport, Massachusetts, she became a talented cartoonist. Terry created the popular comic strip 'Teena' and broke barriers as the first woman to join the National Cartoonists Society. Her artistic flair and determination opened doors for future generations of female artists in the world of comics.
activity_question: What comic strip did Hilda Terry create?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it was important for Hilda Terry to be the first woman in the National Cartoonists Society?

title: France's Rock 'n' Roll Legend Born
story: A baby boy named Jean-Philippe Smet cried his first notes in Paris on June 15, 1943. Little did anyone know he'd grow up to become Johnny Hallyday, France's biggest rock star! Fans called him the 'Elvis Presley of France' because of his powerful voice and energetic performances. Hallyday rocked stages for over half a century, thrilling audiences with his music and acting.
activity_question: What nickname did fans give Johnny Hallyday?
bonus_fact: Johnny Hallyday's real name was Jean-Philippe Smet, but he chose a stage name for his music career.

title: Rock Star Noddy Holder Enters the World
story: A future rock legend took his first breath in Walsall, England. Neville Holder, better known as Noddy, was born in 1946. He would grow up to become the powerful voice and creative force behind the band Slade. Noddy's distinctive vocals and songwriting skills helped Slade create unforgettable hits. June 15 marks the arrival of this British rock icon.
activity_question: What was Noddy Holder's role in the band Slade?
bonus_fact: Noddy Holder's real first name is Neville.

title: Dusty Baker: From All-Star to World Series Champ
story: Dusty Baker swung into the world on June 15, 1949, in Riverside, California. This baseball legend's journey took him from All-Star outfielder to celebrated manager. Baker's talent shone on the field, earning him MLB All-Star honors. Later, he guided teams like the Giants and Astros from the dugout, clinching Manager of the Year awards and a World Series victory.
activity_question: What position did Dusty Baker play before becoming a manager?
follow_up_question: Can you name another team Dusty Baker managed besides the Astros?
